issues.description:
Impact

- 15,000+ active customers are unable to access their wallets in GNC.

- Customers cannot view wallet balances, or use their balances to make purchases, or manage payment methods.

- Support ticket volume is increasing due to this issue.

Expected behaviour

- Customers should be able to view their wallet successfully without any errors.

Actual behaviour

- The system throws a NetDispatcherFaultException when attempting to access the wallet, indicating a deserialization failure due to missing enum values.

Steps to reproduce

- Navigate to the GNC application.

- Search for customer number 5420055 (or any affected customer).

- Attempt to view the customer wallet.

- Observe the error message related to enum deserialization.

Other information

- Error Message:

System.ServiceModel.Dispatcher.NetDispatcherFaultException: The formatter threw an exception while trying to deserialize the message: There was an error while trying to deserialize parameter http://services.golfnow.com/customer:GetCustomerWalletResult. The InnerException message was 'Invalid enum value 'PaymentMethodKey' cannot be deserialized into type 'Golf.RMS.CustService.PaymentType'.

- Missing PaymentType enum values:

- ApplePay = 17

- BakktPay = 18

- PaymentMethodKey = 19

- Related Support Ticket: TTSUPPORT-7257

---
See FULL .md file with full Root Cause Analysis below:

issues.description:
We need an API that will allow front end clients to submit refunds with a cancellation of a product. This is to support new laws related to products with price increases. We already have API2.1 endpoint for cancellation and we have internal service APIs for refunding.

Related APIs

- {{api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/subscription/2448350/cancel?cancelSubscriptionIfBenefitsHaveBeenUsed=true

- {{ProductServiceURL}}/SubscriptionService.svc/rest/subscription/adjust

Requirements

- Ability to cancel a subscription

- Ability to cancel a subscription even if benefits have been used

- Ability to fully refund a subscription

- Ability to partially refund a subscription

- This is a “nice to have feature” but since its already supported on InternalService, figured we could future proof it

- Cancellation should trigger a cancellation email that includes refund details

- Cancellation and refund should update in database and GNC History widget

Refund Types:

Full refund =0

Partial = 1

For full refunds RefundAmount is not required

issues.description:
There is a new law that states when the price increases of a renewing product, we must give the customer 15 days post-renewal to fully cancel and refund their subscription. This ticket is to allow the front end clients to know when to display the button.

- Probably best to add a field to the response of this endpoint:

-  https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/subscriptions?productTypeId=9

Requirements

- Create a boolean flag for something like “IsEligibleForCancellation”

- Eligibility for Cancellation/Refund

- Active subscriptions

- ProductTypeId=9

- RenewedInto ProductId IN (71)

- This should be configurable because new ProductIds may be added later

- The subscription was created as part of a renewal - Automatic renewal through the processor or manual through GNC

- Manually renewing through GNC is not eligible for cancellations

- Billing State/Prov is in a configured state

- New York will be the only one for now but this could expand to multiple state or multiple countries

- Today’s date is <= 15 days since the purchase/transaction date of the subscription

- This day limit should be configurable

Test Cases

- Happy Path for Eligibility

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 3/1/26

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=True

- Not a valid State or date

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 12/31/25

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in Florida

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Not within 15 day range

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 12/31/25

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Not an applicable renewal PPID

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 36 on purchase/transaction date 3/1/26

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Not an applicable transaction - Not a renewal

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I did a standalone purchase of a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 3/6/26

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Subscription already cancelled or expired

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

issues.summary: Move Fee-Messaging to Post-Fee-Calculation & Use FeeConfiguration (API 2.1)
issues.description:
The original story proposed adding a new stored procedure + API toggle to decouple fee retrieval from the TeeTime stored procedure. After discussion with @Daniel McNaughton, we will not change stored procedures. Instead we’ll use the FeeConfiguration module in API 2.1 for this work and ensure fee messaging executes only after the final tee-time transaction fee totals are calculated.

The current problem: Fee Messaging (starting at line ~1727 in TeeTimeRepository.cs) executes too early in the pipeline and therefore does not see the final transaction fee totals. When FeeConfiguration is enabled (or when Reservation.DynamicTransactionFees is modified), the early messaging produces incorrect fee messages.

Goal:

- Identify the place(s) in the request pipeline where all fee calculation logic is completed (including FeeConfiguration module behavior and any Reservation.DynamicTransactionFees overrides).

- Move the fee-messaging process to operate on tee-times after that point so messaging always reflects the final, composed transaction fee totals.

- Use the existing FeeConfiguration module in API 2.1 (no stored proc changes). Default legacy behavior must remain unchanged unless FeeConfiguration is enabled.

Affected endpoints / flows (as called out in the original ticket)

- API 2.1 GetTeeTimes

- API 2.1 GetInvoice

- API 2.1 TeeTimeInvoice

- Reservation Service — Invoice flow
(These endpoints must behave consistently after the change.)

---
Example:

- Revenue team updates trade transaction fees at FID 55 from $3.49 to $1.99

- This update is stored in Reservation.DynamicTransactionFees (not CRMOwnerGolfFacility)

- Current outcome: The API returns $3.49 fee messaging and therefore the UI displays this incorrect value

- Expected outcome: The API should return the $1.99 fee messaging in order for front end to display the correct value

Note: The transaction fee is correct at the invoice level, it’s just the Fee Messaging that needs updated

API results:

UI results:

---

Acceptance Criteria

- No stored proc changes. The database stored procedures remain unchanged. Fee retrieval is performed via the FeeConfiguration module in API 2.1 when enabled.

- Fee-messaging runs only after final totals. Fee-messaging logic is moved so it operates on tee-times after the application layer has composed final totals (after FeeConfiguration and any Reservation.DynamicTransactionFees adjustments). Messaging content must match the final totals.

- Behavior parity / safe default. Default behavior (when FeeConfiguration is disabled) remains identical to current production behavior. When FeeConfiguration is enabled, messages reflect fee totals produced by the FeeConfiguration module + any Revenue.DynamicTransactionFees overrides.

- All affected endpoints updated. GetTeeTimes, GetInvoice, TeeTimeInvoice, and Reservation Service invoice flows all produce correct fee messaging across both legacy and FeeConfiguration-enabled modes.

---
Technical Notes / Implementation Guidance

- Investigation phase

- Locate the fee-messaging implementation referenced by Daniel — currently beginning at ~line 1727 in TeeTimeRepository.cs. Review how and when it runs relative to fee composition.

- Identify every place in the code path where transaction fees are finalized/aggregated for the affected endpoints (for example, final composition in application layer or where invoice totals are built). This may be inside TeeTimeRepository post-processing, invoice composition routines, or Reservation Service invoice flow.

- Identify where FeeConfiguration module is invoked for API 2.1 calls (and where FeeConfiguration may alter totals). Confirm how FeeConfiguration combines with Reservation.DynamicTransactionFees.

- Move/Refactor fee-messaging

- Extract/encapsulate the fee-messaging code so it can be invoked after final totals are composed. The new invocation point should be the single canonical location where the final tee-time totals are guaranteed to be complete (i.e., after any FeeConfiguration logic and any dynamic fee overrides).

- Ensure the message builder references the final totals from the in-memory tee-time objects returned to the API layer (not from intermediate values).

- Where multiple flows produce tee-times (GetTeeTimes, GetInvoice, TeeTimeInvoice, Reservation Service), make sure each flow invokes the new post-composition messaging path.

- FeeConfiguration integration

- When FeeConfiguration is enabled, ensure fee-messaging consumes totals that FeeConfiguration produced (API 2.1 module). If FeeConfiguration is disabled, ensure existing legacy totals and messaging are used.

- Ensure that changes in Reservation.DynamicTransactionFees also reflect in the final totals that the message consumes.

issues.description:
We are seeing an issue on AWS DEV and STG , loading the subscription on the customer profile page in GNC , and we saw this stored procedure is timing out on DataDog :

declare @p2 datetime
set @p2=NULL
exec RmsGetGuestToCustomerConvertDate @CustomerID ='8A383AB1-21E7-409C-9732-9D1D9BE343D3',@GuestToCustomerConvertDate=@p2 output
select @p2

We went into the stored procedure , and the section that is slow is this :

IF ( @ctype = 'Cust' )
BEGIN
SELECT  MIN(audit.CreateDate)
FROM    CRMCustomerAudit audit
WHERE   audit.Details = 'Guest account converted to customer'
AND ( audit.CustomerID = @CustomerIdWithCurlies
OR audit.CustomerID = @CustomerIdWithOutCurlies
)
END

issues.description:
- Merchant Key Mismatch

- When we try to book a teetime that is for a full stack facility. We are generating the PaymentMethodKey using the chargeBy merchantKey, but at the time of transaction, we are doing it with the fullstack merchantKey that is different than the chargeBy. This is causing issues on payment processing. We should use the merchantKey that we are going to use for the transaction to generate the paymentMethodKey. (This will not required changes from CORE side since Payment processing team is going to fix this on their end)

- Guest support

- Currently guests cannot create wallets. This fails the apple pay flow because we need it stored for No Shows. We need to update guest logic to store a wallet ONLY if it is apple pay

- Credit cards should not create a stored wallet for Guest

- Add authorization to the reservation flow

- This already exists in the subscription flow
issues.descriptionHtml:
1. Merchant Key Mismatch

    1. When we try to book a teetime that is for a full stack facility. We are generating the PaymentMethodKey using the chargeBy merchantKey, but at the time of transaction, we are doing it with the fullstack merchantKey that is different than the chargeBy. This is causing issues on payment processing. We should use the merchantKey that we are going to use for the transaction to generate the paymentMethodKey. (This will not required changes from CORE side since Payment processing team is going to fix this on their end)

  2. Guest support

    1. Currently guests cannot create wallets. This fails the apple pay flow because we need it stored for No Shows. We need to update guest logic to store a wallet ONLY if it is apple pay

    2. Credit cards should not create a stored wallet for Guest

  3. Add authorization to the reservation flow

    1. This already exists in the subscription flow

issues.summary: Geosearch API: silent CRM upsert failures and broken Coordinates GeoJSON serialization
issues.description:
Issue

Two related defects were observed on the Geosearch API CRM ingestion path:

- Silent failures on CRM facility upserts. POST /v1/crm/facility returned non-success ResultWrapper responses with no diagnostic logging, making prod failures effectively invisible. ModelState validation failures (HTTP 400) on any controller were also silently rejected with no log line — only the response body told the story.

- Broken Coordinates / GeoJsonPoint serialization on MongoDB driver 2.19+. The driver locked down ObjectSerializer to a default allowlist that excludes the Geosearch.* namespace, so polymorphic / object-typed fields (e.g. Address) failed to serialize. Additionally, Coordinates was being persisted as a legacy POCO { Latitude, Longitude } shape, which:

- Prevented 2dsphere indexes from extracting geo keys (geo queries silently returned nothing).

- Conflicted with the entity-side deserializer that expects GeoJsonPoint<GeoJson2DGeographicCoordinates>, so reads of pre-existing data threw.

Affected endpoints

- POST /v1/crm/facility — CRMController.UpsertCRMFacility (silent failure)

- All controllers — global InvalidModelStateResponseFactory (silent 400s)

- All Mongo write/read paths using Coordinates or Address-typed fields (PoI, GeoLocation, CRM facility, etc.)

Root causes

- No logging hook on UpsertCrmFacilityAsync non-success result, and no logging hook on the API-wide InvalidModelStateResponseFactory.

- MongoDB.Driver 2.19+ ObjectSerializer.DefaultAllowedTypes does not include Geosearch.*.

- Coordinates had no custom serializer; default POCO mapping is incompatible with 2dsphere indexes and with the entity-layer GeoJsonPoint deserializer.

- MongoServiceExtensions.RegisterClassMapping — register a custom ObjectSerializer whose allowlist accepts the framework defaults plus any type whose full name starts with Geosearch..

- New CoordinatesGeoJsonSerializer — persists Coordinates as proper GeoJSON so 2dsphere indexes work.

- New GeoJsonPointTolerantSerializer — reads both legacy { Latitude, Longitude } documents and proper GeoJSON; always writes GeoJSON. Required because existing data is in the legacy shape.

issues.descriptionHtml:
Issue

Two related defects were observed on the Geosearch API CRM ingestion path:

  1. Silent failures on CRM facility upserts. POST /v1/crm/facility returned non-success ResultWrapper responses with no diagnostic logging, making prod failures effectively invisible. ModelState validation failures (HTTP 400) on any controller were also silently rejected with no log line — only the response body told the story.

  2. Broken Coordinates / GeoJsonPoint serialization on MongoDB driver 2.19+. The driver locked down ObjectSerializer to a default allowlist that excludes the Geosearch.* namespace, so polymorphic / object-typed fields (e.g. Address) failed to serialize. Additionally, Coordinates was being persisted as a legacy POCO { Latitude, Longitude } shape, which:

    • Prevented 2dsphere indexes from extracting geo keys (geo queries silently returned nothing).

    • Conflicted with the entity-side deserializer that expects GeoJsonPoint<GeoJson2DGeographicCoordinates>, so reads of pre-existing data threw.

Affected endpoints

  • POST /v1/crm/facility — CRMController.UpsertCRMFacility (silent failure)

  • All controllers — global InvalidModelStateResponseFactory (silent 400s)

  • All Mongo write/read paths using Coordinates or Address-typed fields (PoI, GeoLocation, CRM facility, etc.)

Root causes

  • No logging hook on UpsertCrmFacilityAsync non-success result, and no logging hook on the API-wide InvalidModelStateResponseFactory.

issues.summary: Cache Manager BRS/BRSG fails creating tee times when CRMRackRate.Icons is NULL
issues.description:
ISSUE:
Cache Manager is throwing repeated SQL errors when saving tee time changes:

Procedure or function 'procINSCRMTeeTime_CM' expects parameter '@Icons', which was not supplied.

This appears to happen when CRMRackRate.Icons contains NULL and the related rate period/source logic uses that value while creating or syncing tee times.

---
Impact
The BRS Cache Manager error log is growing rapidly, causing low disk space alerts on the production CM host. If left unresolved, this can impact Cache Manager stability and production sync behavior.

---
Root Cause
Existing CRMRackRate rows had Icons = NULL. When RateSourceCode is changed to INTERFACE or similar source types, Cache Manager assigns:

TeeTime.Icons = Rate.Icons;

That allows a null icon value to flow into CreateTeeTime, where @Icons is added with a null value and SQL Server treats it as not supplied.

---
Temporary Mitigation
Run a data cleanup with rollback backup:

UPDATE dbo.CRMRackRate SET Icons = '' WHERE Icons IS NULL;

This prevents existing null icon values from continuing to trigger the error.

---
Proposed Fix

- Update procINSCRMTeeTime_CM so @Icons defaults to empty string.

- Update Cache Manager to coalesce null icons before calling SQL:

Command.Parameters.AddWithValue("@Icons", icons ?? string.Empty);

---
Acceptance Criteria

- Cache Manager no longer logs @Icons was not supplied.

- Tee time creation succeeds when source icon data is null or empty.

- New CRMRackRate rows with null icons do not break sync.

- BRS/BRSG production logs stop growing abnormally after deployment.

issues.summary: CRMCustomerPhone missing index on CustomerNumber causes timeouts in STG (phx-api-be-internal)
issues.description:
Problem

Datadog APM identified timeouts in the staging environment on service golf365.phx-api-be-internal. Root cause: a query against CRMCustomerPhone filtered by CustomerNumber is falling back to a scan because no supporting index exists on the column. Under STG load this intermittently exceeds the request timeout budget.

Evidence

- Datadog trace: phx-api-be-internal timeout trace (STG)

- Environment: STG

- Service: golf365.phx-api-be-internal

- Table: CRMCustomerPhone

- Column missing index: CustomerNumber

Root Cause

CRMCustomerPhone has no nonclustered index on CustomerNumber. Any stored procedure or ad-hoc query that filters/joins by CustomerNumber (a frequent access pattern) is scanning the table.

Proposed Fix

Create a nonclustered index on CRMCustomerPhone.CustomerNumber. Suggested DDL (final naming/columns to be confirmed during implementation):

CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_CRMCustomerPhone_CustomerNumber
ON CRM.dbo.CRMCustomerPhone (CustomerNumber);

Implementer should verify the exact schema, confirm whether a covering index (including CountryCode, PhoneNumber) is warranted based on the query shape from the Datadog trace, and validate against the stale DDL in CORE-12648 before finalizing.

Acceptance Criteria

- Nonclustered index on CRMCustomerPhone.CustomerNumber exists in STG and PRD.

- Re-run of the failing request flow in STG completes under the timeout threshold.

- Datadog APM shows no recurrence of the scan-induced timeout on phx-api-be-internal for the affected operation over 24 hours post-deploy.

- SQL script added to the appropriate deployment repo/pipeline.

Notes

- Related (informational, not linked): CORE-12647 (epic), CORE-12648 (refactor story — description DDL is stale).

- No GNDS ticket being opened; SQL deployment handled within this ticket.

Note: Built with Claude assistance (claude-opus-4-7)

issues.summary: CAKE Affiliate Tracking - API Response Validation Failure
issues.description:
Impact

- 15,000+ active customers cannot be tracked for CAKE affiliate conversions

- No CAKE conversion tracking between March 11, 2026 and April 21, 2026 (~6 weeks of data loss)

- Lost visibility into affiliate marketing effectiveness for all CAKE network partners

- Potential commission/payment reconciliation issues

- Inaccurate ROI reporting for affiliate campaigns

- All CAKE affiliates affected, including Supreme Golf and other network partners

Expected behaviour

- CAKE affiliate conversion tracking should successfully record conversions in CRM.dbo.CRMAffNetworkReservations

- Successful CAKE API responses should be recognized and processed correctly

- Downstream pipeline (Glue → SSIS → Raptor) should receive new conversion data daily

- Tableau reports should reflect current affiliate conversion activity

Actual behaviour

- CAKE API calls succeed (HTTP 200 OK) but response validation fails

- No records are written to CRM.dbo.CRMAffNetworkReservations database

- Downstream pipeline has no new data to process

- Tableau CAKE report shows stale data (last entry: March 11, 2026)

Steps to reproduce

- Navigate to GolfNow and make a reservation via a CAKE affiliate link (e.g., Supreme Golf)

- Complete the booking process with valid payment

- Check CRM.dbo.CRMAffNetworkReservations table for the reservation ID

- Observe that no record is created (expected behavior: record should exist)

- Test with CAKE tracking URL directly:

https://trk.golfpartnernetwork.com/p.ashx?r=REQ_TEST_001&a=789&t=416784211&f=pb&e=3&ecsk=1806&ecqu=2&ecpr=50.00&ecv=18-COURSE&eccu=USD&ectx=7.50&ecco=US&ecrg=FL&ect=108.50&p=108.50

- Observe response returns Code=1 and Msg=RECEIVED (not the expected Code=0 and Msg=SUCCESS)

Root Cause

File: GolfNow.API.Business/AffiliateNetworks/CakeApi.cs
Method: GetPixelResponse(string url) (lines 19-38)
Method: TrackSaleEcommerce() (lines 128-146)

The CAKE API changed their success response format around March 10, 2026:

Old Format (Expected by our code):

<result>
<code>0</code>
<msg>SUCCESS</msg>
</result>

New Format (Current CAKE API):

<result>
<code>1</code>
<msg>RECEIVED</msg>
</result>

The validation logic at line 296 only accepts Code=0 and Msg=SUCCESS:

if (response.StatusCode == HttpStatusCode.OK && responseMSG != null
&& responseMSG.Code == 0 && responseMSG.Msg == API_MESSAGE)
return true;

When validation fails:

- GetPixelResponse() returns false

- Database write operation at lines 130-145 is skipped

- No record created in CRM.dbo.CRMAffNetworkReservations

Other information

- Last Successful Tracking: March 10, 2026

- Data Gap Period: March 11 - April 21, 2026 (~6 weeks)

- Database Table: CRM.dbo.CRMAffNetworkReservations

- CAKE Tracking Endpoint: https://trk.golfpartnernetwork.com/

- Affected Code Location: DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/AffiliateNetworks/CakeApi.cs

- Related Investigation: DS-22038 - CAKE Report - FactBookingExternalAttribute stale since 2026-03-10

- Root Cause Analysis Document: Full analysis available in attached file

Confirmed Working Components:

- ✅ Front-end: Captures affiliate tracking parameters

- ✅ Reservation API: Receives AffiliateTracking object

- ✅ Workflow Engine: TrackReservationConversionActivity executes

- ✅ CAKE API Call: HTTP request succeeds (200 OK)

- ❌ Response Validation: Fails due to changed CAKE response format

- ⬜ Database Write: Skipped (never reached)

- ✅ Glue Job: Functional (no data to process)

- ✅ SSIS Package: Functional (no data to process)

- ✅ Tableau Report: Reflects stale data accurately

Proposed Solution

Option 1: Update Validation Logic (Recommended)

- Accept Code=1 with Msg=RECEIVED as valid success response

- Accept Code=1 with Msg=DUPLICATED for duplicate tracking (already tracked)

- Keep backward compatibility with Code=0, Msg=SUCCESS if CAKE still supports it

- Add logging to track different response types

Option 2: Defensive Programming

- Treat any HTTP 200 response as success unless explicitly an error

- Log all responses for monitoring

- Alert on unexpected response formats

Data Backfill Required

Once fix is deployed, backfill missing data:

- Use CAKE Conversions reporting API to retrieve March 11 - April 21, 2026 data

- Populate CRM.dbo.CRMAffNetworkReservations via script

- Run Glue job and SSIS package to populate Raptor.dbo.FactBookingExternalAttribute

- Verify Tableau report reflects complete data

issues.description:
Overview

End-to-end QA testing of the Timelyst Inventory & Booking application. Many recent tickets involve foundational changes (refactoring, data schema migrations, architectural improvements) that are not individually QA-testable. Since Timelyst has no production traffic yet, this ticket consolidates full-stack validation into a single QA effort covering data sync, facility discovery, and booking workflows.

Environments:

- Timelyst API (Dev): https://timelyst.dev.golfnow.io

- Timelyst API (Stg): https://timelyst.stg.golfnow.io

- Hangfire Dashboard (Dev): timelyst-hangfire-dev.golfnowdmz.local/hangfire/recurring (accessible via AWS Workspace)

- Hangfire Dashboard (Stg): timelyst-hangfire-stg.golfnowdmz.local/hangfire/recurring (accessible via AWS Workspace)

- API Docs: Swagger / Scalar available at the API base URL

---
Test Steps

Step 1 — Configure Interface in CRM

- In the CRM database, create (or verify) an interface configuration for a golf course with a USC and/or CCH provider.

- Ensure the InterfaceAttribute collection has the correct API URL and credentials for the chosen provider(s).

- Note: CCH credentials are stored in the MongoDB InterfaceAttribute collection (synced from CRM via InterfaceAttributeSyncJob), NOT in GCCourseCode.

Step 2 — Wait for CRM → MongoDB Data Sync

- Wait approximately 1 hour for the recurring Hangfire jobs to auto-sync data from the CRM database to the MongoDB Timelyst database.

- Monitor sync progress on the Hangfire recurring jobs dashboard:
http://timelyst-hangfire-dev.golfnowdmz.local/hangfire/recurring

- Verify that Facility, Offering, Service, and InterfaceAttribute data has been synced into MongoDB.

NOTE: Full-sync jobs will attempt to fetch the full CRM table and sync into Mongo, which should be used for initialization. Incremental-sync jobs will only compare the TimeStamp field with the last sync time and only sync records with the updated TimeStamp field.

Step 3 — Create Interface Configuration via API

Using the Timelyst Management API, create interface configurations for USC and CCH providers (if they don't already exist from the sync).

Action

Method

Endpoint

List existing configs

GET

/api/v1/management/interfaceconfiguration

Create new config

POST

/api/v1/management/interfaceconfiguration

Get config by ID

GET

/api/v1/management/interfaceconfiguration/{id}

Update config

PUT

/api/v1/management/interfaceconfiguration/{id}

Delete config

DELETE

/api/v1/management/interfaceconfiguration/{id}

Validation: Confirm configs are created with correct provider type, facility mapping, and enabled status.

Step 4 — Create Product Verticals & Assign to Offerings

Product verticals classify offerings (e.g., "Golf", "Simulator", "Fitting"). The Value is auto-incremented — no need to pass it in the request body.

4a — Create Product Verticals

Action

Method

Endpoint

List product verticals

GET

/api/v1/management/productvertical

Get by ID

GET

/api/v1/management/productvertical/{id}

Create product vertical

POST

/api/v1/management/productvertical

Update product vertical

PUT

/api/v1/management/productvertical/{id}

Delete product vertical

DELETE

/api/v1/management/productvertical/{id}

Activate

POST

/api/v1/management/productvertical/{id}/activate

Deactivate

POST

/api/v1/management/productvertical/{id}/deactivate

Create request body: { "name": "Simulator" } (Value auto-assigned as max + 1)

Validation:

- Confirm Value is auto-incremented (1, 2, 3, ...)

- Confirm Name must be unique

- Confirm Value is immutable after creation (not editable via update)

4b — Assign Product Vertical to Offering

Assigns a product vertical to an existing offering using the ProductVertical's integer Value and the Offering's CRM GolfCourseID.

Action

Method

Endpoint

Assign to offering

POST

/api/v1/management/productvertical/{value}/assign/{offeringCrmId}

Example: POST /api/v1/management/productvertical/1/assign/12951

Validation:

- Confirm the response returns the updated OfferingDto with productVerticalType set

- Confirm assigning an inactive product vertical returns 400

- Confirm non-existent Value or CRM ID returns 404

- Verify the offering in MongoDB shows updated ProductVerticalType field

Step 5 — Create Service Categories & Assign to Services

Service categories use a power-of-2 bitmask pattern (CategoryId = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16...) to support multi-category assignment on services. Create categories first, then assign them to services synced in Step 2.

5a — Create Service Categories

Action

Method

Endpoint

List categories

GET

/api/v1/management/servicecategory

Get category by ID

GET

/api/v1/management/servicecategory/{id}

Create category

POST

/api/v1/management/servicecategory

Delete category

DELETE

/api/v1/management/servicecategory/{id}

Activate category

POST

/api/v1/management/servicecategory/{id}/activate

Deactivate category

POST

/api/v1/management/servicecategory/{id}/deactivate

Example: Create categories like "FullBag" (CategoryId=1), "Irons" (CategoryId=2), "Putter" (CategoryId=4).

Validation:

- Confirm CategoryId must be a unique power of 2

- Confirm Name must be unique

- Confirm categories cannot be modified after creation (immutable — only activate/deactivate/delete)

5b — Assign Categories to Services

Action

Method

Endpoint

Assign categories

POST

/api/v1/management/service/{id}/categories

Request body: { "serviceCategoryIds": [1, 2] }

Multiple categories are combined via OR bitmask (e.g., assigning IDs 1 & 2 sets CategoryId = 3, Category = "FullBag & Irons").

Validation:

- Confirm the service response shows the combined categoryId bitmask and concatenated category name

- Passing an empty array clears category assignments

- Verify assigned categories persist by re-fetching the service via GET /api/v1/management/service/{id}

Step 6 — Bootstrap & Run Reconciliation Jobs

- Trigger the cm-interface-config-bootstrap job in the Hangfire recurring jobs dashboard and refresh the page.

- Verify that two new recurring jobs are created: cm-recon-cch and cm-recon-usc.

- Run each cm-recon-* job. This should create facility-specific jobs following the pattern: cm-usc-f{facilityId}-c{courseId}-d{day}-{sequence} (e.g., cm-usc-f2134-c12951-d0-1).

- These jobs are only created if the CRM golf course is configured with the corresponding interface provider.

- Manually trigger these facility-specific jobs to sync inventory data with the upstream provider (Club Champion or USchedule).

- Verify that after the jobs complete, resource availability data is populated in MongoDB.

Step 7 — Rebuild Discovery Documents & Verify ServiceCategory in Search

After categories are assigned (Step 5) and inventory is synced (Step 6), trigger the Hangfire job that rebuilds the FacilityDiscovery documents so that category data is indexed for search.

- Trigger the facility-discovery-rebuild job (or equivalent) from the Hangfire dashboard.

- Verify the FacilityDiscovery documents in MongoDB contain ServiceCategoryIds arrays on the service entries:

- Each service should list the decomposed power-of-2 category IDs (e.g., bitmask 7 → [1, 2, 4])

- Test search with serviceCategoryIds filter:

#

Action

Method

Endpoint

Key Validations

1

Search by category

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/search?serviceCategoryIds={id}

Only facilities with services matching the category are returned

2

Search by multiple categories

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/search?serviceCategoryIds={id1}&serviceCategoryIds={id2}

Facilities with services matching ANY of the provided categories are returned

3

Detail search by category

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/search/detail?serviceCategoryIds={id}

Full search with services filtered to matching categories; verify serviceCategoryIds array in response

4

Search without filter

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/search

All facilities returned regardless of category

Validation:

- Services in the response include serviceCategoryIds arrays with correct decomposed values

- Filtering by a category that no service has returns zero results

- The category string field on services shows the human-readable concatenated name

Step 8 — Test Client API (Facility Discovery)

Test the 5 client-facing facility discovery endpoints and validate responses.

#

Action

Method

Endpoint

Key Validations

1

Search facilities

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/search

Returns facilities matching search/geo criteria; check latitude, longitude, productVerticalType filters

2

Get facility summary

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/facilities/{facilityId}/summary

Returns basic facility info (amenities, operating hours, timezone); no offerings/services

3

Get facility detail

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/facilities/{facilityId}/detail

Returns full hierarchy (offerings → services); no date-specific availability

4

Get facility availability

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/facilities/{facilityId}/availability?date={yyyyMMdd}

Returns services with availability calendar for the specified date; verify timeslots are present

5

Search with detail

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/search/detail

Full search with services, rate sets, and timeslots; validate pricing and availability

Validation: Confirm that facilities synced in Steps 2–6 appear in search results with correct offerings, services, and availability data.

Step 9 — Test Booking & Cancellation

#

Action

Method

Endpoint

Key Validations

1

Get supported providers

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/management/booking/providers

Returns list of supported providers (USC, CCH)

2

Make a reservation

POST

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/management/booking/reserve?provider={USC\|CCH}

Booking succeeds; confirmation number returned

3

Verify booking reflected

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/facilities/{facilityId}/availability?date={yyyyMMdd}

The booked timeslot shows as unavailable / reduced capacity

4

Cancel the reservation

POST

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/management/booking/cancel?provider={USC\|CCH}

Cancellation succeeds using confirmation number

5

Verify cancellation reflected

GET

/api/v1/channels/{channelId}/client/facilitydiscovery/facilities/{facilityId}/availability?date={yyyyMMdd}

The previously booked timeslot is available again

Validation: After booking, the availability endpoint should reflect the consumed timeslot. After cancellation, the timeslot should be restored.

---
Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CRM → MongoDB data sync completes successfully via Hangfire jobs

- [ ] Interface configurations can be created/read/updated/deleted via the Management API

- [ ] Product verticals can be created (with auto-incremented Value), listed, updated, activated, deactivated, and deleted

- [ ] Product verticals can be assigned to offerings via the assign endpoint using Value and CRM ID

- [ ] Service categories can be created, listed, activated, deactivated, and deleted

- [ ] Service categories can be assigned to services and the bitmask/name is correctly computed

- [ ] Bootstrap job creates the expected reconciliation jobs (cm-recon-cch, cm-recon-usc)

- [ ] Reconciliation jobs create facility-specific sync jobs and successfully sync upstream provider data

- [ ] FacilityDiscovery documents contain ServiceCategoryIds after rebuild

- [ ] Search endpoints correctly filter by serviceCategoryIds query parameter

- [ ] All 5 client facility discovery endpoints return correct data including category information

- [ ] Booking endpoint successfully creates a reservation and timeslot availability is updated

- [ ] Cancellation endpoint successfully cancels a reservation and timeslot availability is restored

Method

Endpoint

issues.summary: True-Up Not Switching to TSP Upon AL Deactivation
issues.description:
Support ticket: TTSUPPORT-7299

Based on investigation:

- TSP (Tee Sheet Pricing) is enabled

- AL (Autoload) is deactivated

- Tee times are blocked

- XCS Stage shows no inventory

- CM appears to still attempt to use AL inventory (which is empty)

- TSP inventory is not being surfaced

This behavior previously worked (early fall) and appears to have regressed, potentially after AWS-related changes or True-Up updates. There is a known workaround to create a new rate set and that will resolve any syncing issues since there are no historical RackTimes or TeeTimes to misidentify.

---
Expected Behavior

When:

- TSP is enabled and

- AL is deactivated

➡️ The system should:

- Fully switch to TSP inventory

- Surface available inventory correctly in XCS Stage

- Not attempt to reference AL

---
Actual Behavior

- System continues referencing AL inventory even when it is deactivated

- No inventory is returned

- TSP inventory is ignored

---
Key Question / Hypothesis

True-Up logic may still be “holding on” to AL as the inventory source instead of properly falling back to or prioritizing TSP.

---
Scope of Investigation

- Review True-Up inventory selection logic

- Validate fallback conditions when AL is disabled

- Identify any regression introduced post-AWS migration

- Check for:

- Cached inventory source selection

- Feature flags or config mismatches

- Conditional logic prioritizing AL incorrectly

---
Acceptance Criteria

- When AL is deactivated, system does not attempt to use AL inventory

- TSP inventory is correctly selected and returned

- Inventory appears correctly in XCS Stage

- No regression for cases where AL is active

- Behavior matches pre-AWS / early fall functionality

---
Testing Steps

- Set Rate Set to NOT Tee Sheet Pricing

- Make sure the the rate set has a CRMRackPeriod with RateSourceCode=NONE

- Should should just be to create a rate fence if it doesnt already exist

- Run an autoload for that rate set

- Manually block all the tee times

- Run XCS or True up/CM to get data populated in XCS Stage and Rack tables

- Change the interface to use Tee Sheet Pricing

- Run TrueUp/CM again and make sure that Rack Price matches the value from the tee sheet

- THis was being set to $0 when it was broken

issues.summary: Use LaunchDarkly for configuration + accept FacilityId and ChannelId inputs
issues.description:
We need to replace the current web.config-based toggling for the FeeConfiguration code with a server-side LaunchDarkly feature flag. The goal is to be able to remotely enable the FeeConfiguration behavior in production for testing without editing web.config or temporarily hard-coding IDs.

This work supports the larger change to move fee messaging to run after final fee totals are composed (https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14117#icft=CORE-14117). This ticket focuses only on the feature-flagging and configuration input portion: enable FeeConfiguration through LaunchDarkly.

Acceptance Criteria

- LaunchDarkly flag created

- A server-side LaunchDarkly feature flag is created

- Behavior and fallback

- Default behavior remains legacy (FeeConfiguration disabled) unless the LaunchDarkly flag enables it.

- While migrating, if LaunchDarkly is unreachable for any reason, the code should fall back to the existing CRMConfig value (so production safety is maintained). A separate follow-up can remove the fallback after rollout.

- Operational readiness

- Logging of some sort includes whether FeeConfiguration was enabled by LaunchDarkly and the facilityId/channelId used for evaluation

NOTE: This will connect to both API 2.1 and Reservation Service

Removed the FacilityId/ChannelId context key from this ticket, will consider implementing in a future ticket. - @Daniel McNaughton

issues.summary: Integrate New EZL Contact Endpoint into NEW EZL Booking Flow
issues.description:
Summary

As part of the initiative to reduce EZL1 errors caused by contact-related failures, we need to replace the current SOAP-based contact lookup/creation logic in the new (non-legacy) EZLinks booking flow with calls to the new EZL contact REST endpoint introduced in RTS-5547. This change is scoped exclusively to the new API booking path — the legacy booking API (MakeReservationOldAPI) is not affected.

---
Background / Context

EZL1 errors have been occurring due to failures in contact creation and association during the EZLinks booking flow. RTS-5547 (owned by the EZLinks team) introduced a new REST endpoint for contact handling that is expected to resolve these failures.

In the current implementation, contact operations in the new booking flow still rely on legacy SOAP methods:

- Contact lookup: UserGetRegistration (SOAP) via GetEZLCustomerContactId()

- Contact creation: AddMember (SOAP) via BuildNewCustomerParams()

These legacy SOAP calls are the suspected source of the EZL1 contact errors. The new REST endpoint introduced in RTS-5547 should replace these calls in the new booking path only.

The key entry point is MakeReservation() in Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/EZLinksTeeSheet.cs. The flow branches at line ~84:

var useLegacyReservationsApi = request.TeeTime.RateSet?.Settings?.UseLegacyReservationsAPI ?? false;
if (useLegacyReservationsApi)
return this.MakeReservationOldAPI(request); // DO NOT TOUCH
// New REST API flow continues below — this is our scope

The contact_id returned from the EZL contact endpoint is passed into each booking slot in the REST payload via BuildEzLinksBookingSlots(), so accurate contact resolution is critical for a successful booking submission to POST /v1/tee-time/book.

---
Problem Statement

The current new booking API flow uses legacy SOAP methods for contact lookup and creation, which are the root cause of recurring EZL1 errors. Specifically:

- Improper or missing contact records in EZL result in invalid contact_id values

- An invalid contact_id in the booking slot payload causes downstream EZL1 failures when calling POST /v1/tee-time/book

- The SOAP AddMember and UserGetRegistration calls are unreliable under certain conditions (e.g., duplicate detection edge cases, credential casing issues)

---
Scope

In scope:

- Replace SOAP-based contact lookup (GetEZLCustomerContactId / UserGetRegistration) with the new EZL contact REST endpoint in the new booking flow

- Replace SOAP-based contact creation (BuildNewCustomerParams / AddMember) with the new EZL contact REST endpoint in the new booking flow

- Ensure the contact_id returned by the new endpoint is correctly passed to BuildEzLinksBookingSlots() and the POST /v1/tee-time/book payload

Out of scope:

- MakeReservationOldAPI() — legacy booking path must not be changed

- Any non-EZLinks tee sheet providers

- Any EZRTS flows not using the new REST API booking path

---
Acceptance Criteria

Functional:

- In the new booking API flow (when UseLegacyReservationsAPI = false), contact lookup/creation calls the new EZL REST contact endpoint (per RTS-5547 contract) instead of SOAP

- A valid contact_id from the new endpoint is used to populate the player slot in the booking payload sent to POST /v1/tee-time/book

- Contact is either created or retrieved and associated correctly — no duplicate contacts are created

- The legacy MakeReservationOldAPI() path is completely unchanged

Error Handling:

- Failures from the new contact endpoint are caught and wrapped in an appropriate TeeSheetException (consistent with existing pattern in EZLinksTeeSheet.cs)

- Logging added for:

- Contact endpoint call (request/response at DEBUG level)

- Contact creation vs. retrieval outcome

- Contact endpoint failures (at ERROR level with full context: customer ID, course, error message)

- Any mapping failures between EZL contact response and booking slot schema

- If the new contact endpoint returns an error equivalent to the existing ERR_INVALID_CONTACT_ID (error code 40) or similar, behavior should follow the same retry/fallback pattern already in place

Integration:

- Change is gated by the UseLegacyReservationsAPI = false branch — no impact to legacy flow

- New REST contact endpoint call uses the same EZLinksConfiguration (credentials, RestApiUrl) already loaded in MakeReservation()

- Bearer token auth pattern (consistent with GetAuthorizationTokenAsync() → POST /v1/auth) is used if required by the new endpoint

---
Technical Implementation Guidance

Primary file: Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/EZLinksTeeSheet.cs

The contact block to replace lives in MakeReservation() approximately at lines 114–165. Currently:

- GetEZLCustomerContactId() → SOAP UserGetRegistration → returns contactId

- If contactId < 1: BuildNewCustomerParams() → SOAP AddMember → returns contactId

Replace with:

- Call new EZL contact REST endpoint (contract from RTS-5547) to create or retrieve the contact

- Extract the contact_id from the response

- Pass the contact_id into the existing BuildEzLinksBookingSlots() call (the rest of the booking flow is unchanged)

Relevant models to reference or extend:

- Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/Contracts/Player.cs — ContactId field consumed in slot building

- Internal Services/GolfNow.Services.TeeSheet/GolfNow.Services.TeeSheet.Common/Model/TeeSheetCustomer.cs — source of customer fields (name, email, phone, address) to send to the new endpoint

- Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/Model/EZLinksError.cs — add any new error codes introduced by RTS-5547

New request/response contracts for the contact endpoint should be added alongside the existing contracts in Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/Contracts/.

Idempotency note: The new endpoint should handle create-or-retrieve semantics. Confirm with the EZLinks team (RTS-5547) whether the endpoint is idempotent on email/customer ID to avoid duplicate contact creation (previously guarded against via ERR_DUPLICATE_INTERNET_USER_ID and ERR_DUPLICATE_MEMBERSHIP_NUMBER handling in SOAP flow).

---
Dependencies

- RTS-5547 — EZLinks team must deliver and document the new contact endpoint (REST contract, auth requirements, field mapping) before CORE implementation begins

- Confirm whether the new contact endpoint requires the same Bearer token auth flow as POST /v1/tee-time/book, or separate credentials

---
The main implementation change is contained entirely within EZLinksTeeSheet.cs — the contact block in MakeReservation() (new-flow branch only). Everything from TeeTimeValidate() onward in the booking chain remains untouched.

Please Contact Sarah Wolfkiel to get in touch with their QA team for testing coorindation
